title: Sensitive Data Exposed In JavaScript
description: Replay validation detected token- or secret-like material in client-side JavaScript.
impact: Client-side secrets can enable API abuse, credential replay, or broader environment compromise.
likelihood: High when externally served JavaScript contains reusable secrets or privileged tokens.
recommendations: Remove secrets from client-side code and move sensitive operations server-side.
Rotate any exposed tokens or credentials immediately.
Review build pipelines to prevent accidental client-side secret inclusion.
